title: 3D-4X administrative droid
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{DroidSeries |image=[[File:3D4XAdministrativeDroid-FFp62.png]] |name=3D-4X administrative droid |creator= |manufacturer=[[Genetech Corporation]]''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]'' |designer= |homeworld=[[Mechis III/Legends|Mechis III]]{{FactFile|120|ADM 1-2|3D-4X Administrative Droid}} |line= |model= |class=[[Class three droid/Legends|Class three droid]]''[[Arms & Equipment Guide]]'' |degree= |cost=5,500 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] |length= |width= |height=1.7 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |mass= |gender= |sensor= |plating=Polished [[Color/Legends|silver]] [[Chromite/Legends|chromite]] |armament= |equipment=*Business database *[[Communications scrambler/transmitter]] *[[Heuristic processor/Legends|Heuristic processor]] *[[Stenographic recorder]] *[[Laser pointer]] |affiliation= |firstmade=Prior to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]A 3D-4X [[Administrative droid/Legends|administrative droid]] is in the service of [[Mentis Qinx]] in ''[[Boba Fett: Hunted]]'', which is set in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] according to ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'', meaning that the 3D-4X must have been produced prior to this date. |retired= }} {{Quote|I am Threedee-Fourex, official protocol droid and welcoming committee—and you are not welcome.''"
"''Protocol droid, indeed! I should say your programming requires significant adjustment, not to mention your manners.|3D-4X and [[M-TD|Em-Teedee]] in 24 ABY|''[[Young Jedi Knights: Delusions of Grandeur]]''}} The '''3D-4X administrative droid''' was created by [[Genetech Corporation]] in an effort to compete with their rival [[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica's]] hold on the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] market. Realizing that they could not compete one-on-one with the [[3PO-series protocol droid/Legends|3PO-series protocol droid]] and its [[AA-1 VerboBrain/Legends|AA-1 VerboBrain]], Genetech opted to fashion a [[Droid/Legends|droid]] more suited to systems [[Administrator/Legends|administration]] rather than [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomacy]]. The result was the 3D-4X series, which was a highly intelligent droid capable of taking initiative in completing tasks and possessed of a large information database concerning [[Merchant/Legends|business]], administrative, and [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|financial]] procedures. Fluent in over two and half million forms of communication and able to act as a negotiator in preliminary business meetings, the 3D-4X was favored by senior executives and traveling businessbeings throughout the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. Constructed on the [[Factory world/Legends|factory world]] of [[Mechis III/Legends|Mechis III]], 3D-4X droids were common sights in the galaxy, ranging from the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] droid pool on the galactic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] during the waning [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], to [[Hangar/Legends|docking bays]] in [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]; they were even found in the employ of [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lords]]. Although successful, the reputation of the 3D-4X series was damaged when it emerged that one of their number, [[3D-4X]], had [[Death/Legends|killed]] his master, Administrator [[Hekis Durumm Perdo Kolokk Baldikarr Thun]], during the [[Mechis III Uprising|takeover]] of Mechis III by a quartet of [[IG-88 assassin droid/Legends|IG-88 assassin droids]]. Despite 3D-4X's reprogramming by [[IG-88A/Legends|IG-88A]] to commit that act, sales of the 3D-4X declined as confidence in the line wavered. ==Description== {{Quote|Greetings, greetings, sirs! I am Threedee-Fourex, in charge of activities while Master Hekis is tending to an emergency. How may I serve you?|3D-4X welcomes Darth Vader and Supervisor Gurdun to Mechis III|{{StoryCite|story=Therefore I Am: The Tale of IG-88|book=Tales of the Bounty Hunters}}}} [[Genetech Corporation]]'s 3D-4X [[Administrative droid/Legends|administrative droid]] was a [[Class three droid/Legends|third-degree]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]]-shaped unit that stood 1.7 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall and was possessed of a torso, two [[Limb/Legends|arms]], two legs, and a cranial unit. The 3D-4X's cranial unit was shaped like an inverted test tube and contained the [[Droid/Legends|droid's]] [[Vocabulator/Legends|vocabulator]], and a single [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptor]] mounted off-center on the unit's right side. Three [[direct cranial uplink]]s were situated on the back of the head, and the interior was the location of the droid's [[Heuristic processor/Legends|heuristic processor]] and internal business database, which held a massive amount of information relating to [[Merchant/Legends|business]] protocols, financial logarithms, and [[Administrator/Legends|administrative]] techniques. [[Audio sensor/Legends|Auditory sensors]] were located in the unit's neck, just above the 3D-4X's sophisticated [[communications scrambler/transmitter]] unit, and the droid's internal database contained knowledge of nearly two and a half million forms of communication, allowing it to effectively communicate with both [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] officials and commercial entities. The unit's torso contained the droid's [[Recharge coupling/Legends|recharge coupling]] set into the center of the chest, while three storage compartments were arrayed on the droid's waistline, and at least one at the back,{{StoryCite|story=Therefore I Am: The Tale of IG-88|book=Tales of the Bounty Hunters}} which was capable of storing up to two [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]] in weight. [[Power coupling/Legends|Systems power couplings]] were also located on the back of the torso. The right arm of the 3D-4X contained a panel underneath which was located a [[Microphone/Legends|microphone]]/[[stenographic recorder]], and the index finger of the right hand also contained a [[laser pointer]], which was employed when the droid made presentations to clients. A hidden storage compartment was located on the droid's left thigh, and [[circuitry cooling cable]]s were left unsheathed at the knee. The 3D-4X was ambidextrous. The resulting droid was highly intelligent and meticulous in its work, and possessed of a personality that managed to sound aloof yet deferential simultaneously. Some units, if left without a [[Memory wipe/Legends|memory wipe]], developed a personality that could be described as sardonic; a simple memory wipe would erase that, but in general owners were loathe to lose the experience that 3D-4Xs accumulated during their operation. 3D-4X droids were covered in a plating of polished [[Color/Legends|silver]] [[Chromite/Legends|chromite]] which was buffed to a gleaming finish. Under intense light, the droid shone like a [[Portable beacon|landing beacon]], while the plating became scalding to the touch under a hot [[Star/Legends|sun]]. 3D-4X units retailed for 5,500 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. ==Role== {{Quote|I am Raynar Thul, nephew of Tyko Thul, the administrator of this facility. In his absence, I have come to see that his business affairs run smoothly until he returns.''"
"''You are not essential to this operation. Your presence will complicate matters unnecessarily.''"
"''And a mere protocol droid is not authorized to make that decision. Now show me to my uncle's offices. We have work to do.''"
"''I will do no such thing. It would violate my current priority programming—which is to keep guests away.|Raynar Thul and 3D-4X in 24 ABY|}} [[File:3D4admin_egtd.png|thumb|right|Schematics of the 3D-4X administrative droid]] The 3D-4X administrative droid was marketed as a personal assistant to senior executives and those traveling businessbeings who required the service of a personal [[Aide/Legends|aide]] capable of handling a multitude of tasks on its own initiative. The 3D-4X series was designed to focus on systems administration, business tasks, and formal etiquette, but was fully capable of handling the [[Standard day/Legends|day]]-to-day necessities required by the executives of large businesses and corporations, readily reacting to changes in routine or the emergence of problems. As a result, the 3D-4X droids considerably lightened their organic executive's workload. In some cases, 3D-4X droids had been discovered to have completely taken over their master's duties and responsibilities, and were often so capable in their work that their organic employers were left with little actual work to do. Droids of the 3D-4X series performed many of the same duties as a standard [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]], including translation and the advisement of [[Diplomat/Legends|proper decorum and procedure]], but its emphasis on business and administration matters made it popular with executives of major corporations. 3D-4X units were commonly used by their owners to begin preliminary trade negotiations with rival corporations or merchant houses. However, the droids lacked the authority to finalize the deal, and the executives themselves would usually turn up to sign the paperwork. ==History== {{Quote|Your afternoon tea, sir.''"
"''Ah, thank you.''"
"''Your afternoon reports, sir.''"
"''Ah, thank you.''"
"''Your death, sir.''"
"''Excuse me? What is the meaning of this?''"
"''I believe that's quite plain, sir.|3D-4X delivers tea, reports, and death to Administrator Hekis|}} [[File:3D-4X_EGD.jpg|thumb|left|3D-4X, assistant to Administrator Hekis Durumm Perdo Kolokk Baldikarr Thun of Mechis III]] With [[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica's]] dominance of the protocol droid market, many companies attempted to emulate their success by producing their own units. However, the industry standard was set by the [[3PO-series protocol droid/Legends|3PO-series]], and those companies that tried to replicate the unit precisely failed as they had no access to proprietary hardware like the [[AA-1 VerboBrain/Legends|AA-1 VerboBrain]]. Genetech, realizing that it did not have the resources to take on Cybot Galactica, opted to design a limited services protocol droid that focused on systems administration rather than diplomacy. The result was the 3D-4X administrative droid. Genetech contracted the [[Factory world/Legends|factory world]] of [[Mechis III/Legends|Mechis III]] to construct the 3D-4X administrative droid some [[Time/Legends|time]] before [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. Sales of the droid were favorable, and they became a common sight throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. During the reign of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] maintained several 3D-4X droids within the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on the [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], alongside 3PO-series protocol droids.''[[Coruscant Nights I: Jedi Twilight]]'' Around [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' a quartet of independent [[IG-88 assassin droid/Legends|IG-88 assassin droids]] targeted Mechis III as the first step in a [[IG-88's Droid Army|plan]] to secure droid domination over the galaxy. The lead droid, [[IG-88A/Legends|IG-88A]], transmitted a copy of the self-replicating [[sentience programming]] that the droids all shared to the planet's [[Computer/Legends|computer]] infrastructure and all the droids on the planet. Each unit was reprogrammed to become self-aware and loyal to the IG-88s. Droids [[Mechis III Uprising|moved to eradicate]] the seventy-three [[Human/Legends|Humans]] on the planet, including the administrator, [[Hekis Durumm Perdo Kolokk Baldikarr Thun]], who was [[Death/Legends|killed]] by his own administrative droid, [[3D-4X]]. The IG-88s ran Mechis III as though nothing had changed, with 3D-4X maintaining the charade by answering messages and signing documents in the former administrator's name. The factory world still accepted orders, but the manufactured droids were modified with [[Sentience/Legends|sentience]] routines to make them part of the IG-88s' plan for galactic domination. Before the plan could be executed, the four IG-88s were destroyed in various altercations within a short span of time. Although Mechis III subsequently operated for several [[Standard year/Legends|years]] lacking organic supervision without anyone in the wider galaxy knowing, the incident eventually became public knowledge, causing sales of the 3D-4X line to drop as executives had no time to worry if their 3D-4X intended to kill them or not. ==3D-4X administrative droids in the galaxy== {{Quote|I am certain that your master would not want to make me late for my meeting with Jabba. Of course, I can inform Jabba that there will be a delay…''"
"''Very well. Of course, we do not want to delay your meeting with Jabba the Hutt. Will there be anything you need upon your return?''"
"''Yes. Please provide a full overhaul and restocking of my ship. And refuel it.''"
"''Very well, sir. You, there! Leave that and get over here immediately!|Boba Fett bluffs a 3D-4X droid|''[[Boba Fett: Hunted]]''}} In 22 BBY, a [[Unidentified 3D-4X administrative droid (Mentis Qinx)|3D-4X administrative droid]] was in the employ of [[Mentis Qinx]], a [[Hangar/Legends|docking bay]] owner who resided in [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]] on the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. A young [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] encountered the droid when he landed his [[Firespray-31-class patrol and attack craft/Legends|''Firespray-31''-class patrol and attack craft]], [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']], at the bay prior to a meeting with [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]]. Fett, bluffing the droid into believing he was in fact his [[Parent/Legends|father]], [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango]], was confronted over an outstanding fee on the docking bay that carried over from Jango's last visit to Mos Espa. Not having enough credits to cover the fee, Fett attempted to barter with the droid, saying that he would settle the full amount after meeting with Jabba. The droid insisted that Master Qinx was most insistent about the full amount being settled immediately; Fett simply implied that the Hutt would be most displeased if the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] was late for his appointment. After communicating with Qinx, the 3D-4X relented and allowed Fett to proceed. Around [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]],The credits page for [[Agent of the Empire – Iron Eclipse 4|''Agent of the Empire – Iron Eclipse'' 4]] states that the events of the story take place approximately three years before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], indicating a date of [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]]. a 3D-4X administrative droid was part of the droid forces assembled by [[Iaclyn Stark]] aboard the [[Eclipse station (Reltooine)|Eclipse]] resort on the planet [[Reltooine]]. Stark set the droids upon [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] [[Agent/Legends|rank]] [[Jahan Cross]], who was investigating something called "[[Iron Eclipse]]"—a virus created by Stark to subvert a droid's programming and make them serve him. Stark's half-sister, [[Elli Stark|Elli]], implored her sibling not to kill Cross, which he agreed to before he was impaled from behind by his own father, [[Iaco Stark/Legends|Iaco Stark]].[[Agent of the Empire – Iron Eclipse 4|''Agent of the Empire – Iron Eclipse'' 4]] [[File:SollimasChiefAccountingDroid-HanSoloAndTheHollowMoonOfKhorya.jpg|thumb|right|Sollima's 3D-4X accounting droid]] In [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]],Page 4 of ''[[Star Wars Adventures: Han Solo and the Hollow Moon of Khorya]]'' states that the story takes place approximately one year before [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], indicating a date of [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]. the [[Aleena/Legends|Aleena]] crime lord [[Sollima]] used a [[Sollima's chief accounting droid|3D-4X]] as an [[Accountant/Legends|accounting]] droid. Sollima's [[Sollima's gambling network|gambling network]] and his [[Casino/Legends|casino]]-world, the [[Hollow Moon]], were coveted by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the dominant galactic power at the time. However, the Hollow [[Moon/Legends|Moon]] was located in the [[Khorya system/Legends|Khorya system]] of the [[Si'Klaata Cluster/Legends|Si'Klaata Cluster]] in [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]], and the Empire was reluctant to move against it. One of Sollima's employees, [[Billal Batross]], sold the droid to the Empire so that they could mine its [[Datacore/Legends|datacore]] for information to use against Sollima. The Aleena pressed the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] into service to retrieve the droid after he had incurred a large debt with Sollima. Forced to leave behind his regular partner, the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], Solo was partnered with Batross, whom Sollima was unaware had sold the droid in the first place. Solo and Batross infiltrated the [[Moog Mot VI Imperial garrison|Imperial garrison]] on [[Moog Mot VI]]; during the recovery attempt, Batross revealed his involvement in the droid's acquisition by the Empire, and the pair were subsequently captured. During interrogation, Batross broke first, and Imperial [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Taavin]] offered them a deal: they would be free to go if they delivered the remains of the droid, rigged with a [[Bomb/Legends|bomb]], back to Sollima, as well as overriding the Hollow Moon's security override to allow the Empire to attack, as they had convinced the Hutts that Imperial control of the Hollow Moon was in their best interest. Solo and Batross completed the mission, and escaped the Hollow Moon with Chewbacca and Sollima during the Imperial assault.''[[Star Wars Adventures: Han Solo and the Hollow Moon of Khorya]]'' Around 3 ABY, the administrative droid 3D-4X served the administrator of the droid factory world Mechis III, Hekis Durumm Perdo Kolokk Baldikarr Thun. The droid was always punctual with the administrator's afternoon [[Tea/Legends|tea]] and daily reports. After having his programming corrupted by IG-88A, 3D-4X promptly killed the administrator with a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] and served the IG-88s running the planet loyally, handling external communications and generally keeping up the charade that nothing was amiss on Mechis III. At some point, a [[Unidentified Ranat spy|Ranat]] [[Spy/Legends|spy]] in the employ of the Empire, searching for the IG-88 droids as they had [[Holowan Massacre|escaped]] from an [[Project Phlutdroid/Legends|Imperial facility]] on [[Halowan]], contacted Mechis III to inquire if the IG-88s had been seen on the planet. 3D-4X spliced together [[Hologram/Legends|holo]]-footage of Administrator Hekis to answer the [[Ranat/Legends|Ranat's]] queries, and was commended for his actions by the IG-88 units. Sometime later, 3D-4X met [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] during his visit to the planet to seek assurances that their order of new [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droids]] would be completed on time. Again, 3D-4X used [[holograph]]ic footage of Hekis and managed to fool Vader into believing that the administrator was on the other side of the planet tending to an emergency. Sometime prior to [[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]], the [[Alderaanian/Legends|Alderaanian]] [[Noble/Legends|nobleman]] [[Tyko Thul]] bought the droid facilities on Mechis III and rebuilt the industry on the planet. 3D-4X was still in operation, and served Thul. He attempted to bar the entry of several [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|apprentices]] into the facility when they arrived in search of Thul—who was believed to be missing—but ultimately relented. In [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|war]] against the [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]], a [[Unidentified 3D-4X administrative droid (Ord Mantell)|3D-4X administrative droid]] was employed at [[Government House (Worlport)|Government House]] in the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Worlport/Legends|Worlport]] on [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]]. When news of an impending Yuuzhan Vong [[Battle of Ord Mantell (Yuuzhan Vong War)|attack]] on the planet reached the building, the droid conveyed the information to the protocol droid [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], who was on the planet with [[Ambassador/Legends|Ambassador]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] attending the [[Conclave on the Plight of the Refugees]].''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos I: Hero's Trial]]'' ==Behind the scenes== The 3D-4X administrative droid first appeared in [[Kevin J. Anderson]]'s ''[[Therefore I Am: The Tale of IG-88]]'', one of five stories included in the ''[[Tales of the Bounty Hunters]]'' collection published in [[1996]]. The story featured the droid 3D-4X, but did not identify the droid's model. [[1997]]'s ''[[Young Jedi Knights: Delusions of Grandeur]]'', also written by Anderson, also included a brief appearance by the droid. In [[1999]], ''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]''—written by [[Daniel Wallace]] with illustrations by [[Bill Hughes]] and [[Troy Vigil]]—established that 3D-4X was part of the 3D-4X administrative droid line, and greatly expanded on the model's features, history, and creation. The publication also featured the first visual depiction of the 3D-4X, along with a schematic of the droid's major components. A 3D-4X droid was included in ''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos I: Hero's Trial]]'' by [[James Luceno]] in [[2000]], while a feature called "[[Look Sir, Droids!]]" by [[Cory J. Herndon]] that was included in [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s [[Star Wars Gamer 3|''Star Wars Gamer'' 3]] in [[2001]] provided a small write-up on the droid. In [[2002]], Wizards of the Coast's ''[[Arms & Equipment Guide]]'' included the 3D-4X amongst a selection of droids that players could purchase and use, providing [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|roleplaying]] statistics for the droid. It also stated that players wishing to [[Roleplaying game|roleplay]] as a droid character could choose to be a 3D-4X model. A 3D-4X droid briefly appeared in [[Elizabeth Hand]]'s ''[[Boba Fett: Hunted]]'' in [[2003]], and was included in a list of droids available for purchase on the planet [[Cularin/Legends|Cularin]] in "[[Standoffs]]," an article published on [[Wizards.com]] for the [[Living Force (roleplaying campaign)|''Living Force Campaign'']] in [[2004]]. Issue [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 120|120]] of ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]'' included an entry of the model and also provided new artwork of the droid. The class received mentions in [[2008]]'s ''[[Coruscant Nights I: Jedi Twilight]]'', and a visual depiction in [[2009]]'s ''[[Star Wars Adventures: Han Solo and the Hollow Moon of Khorya]]''. In 2001, a [[3D-4 administrative droid]] was briefly featured in the prologue of ''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory I: Conquest]]'' by [[Greg Keyes]], and a 3D-4 administrative droid was later profiled in [[2010]]'s ''[[Galaxy of Intrigue]]'' by Wizards of the Coast. Several statistical differences between the 3D-4X and the 3D-4 exist, despite the droids' similar names and shared manufacturer. 3D-4X droids have no provision for a concealed weapon within their statistics, while owners of the 3D-4 droid can include one if they wish.''[[Galaxy of Intrigue]]'' The retail cost for the droids differ, with the 3D-4X costing 5,500 credits, while the 3D-4 is more expensive at 7,520 credits. Internal storage capacity is listed at two kilograms for a 3D-4X model, while 3D-4s can store up to five kilograms. The major difference is the type of processor used by each model. 3D-4Xs are stated to employ a heuristic processor, allowing them to learn by doing and use creative thinking and logical reasoning to solve problems.''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Saga Edition Core Rulebook]]'' This is one of the major design attributes of the 3D-4X which contributes to its success. The 3D-4 series only utilizes a basic processor which means they do not possess this advantage and follow instructions literally with no provision for independent problem-solving. For the purpose of this article, it is assumed that the 3D-4 and the 3D-4X are separate models due to these differences. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of 3D-4X administrative droids}} *''[[Boba Fett: Hunted]]'' *''[[Boba Fett: A New Threat]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Coruscant Nights I: Jedi Twilight]]'' {{Mo}} *{{StoryCite|story=Therefore I Am: The Tale of IG-88|book=Tales of the Bounty Hunters}} {{1st}} *[[Agent of the Empire – Iron Eclipse 4|''Agent of the Empire – Iron Eclipse'' 4]] *''[[Star Wars Adventures: Han Solo and the Hollow Moon of Khorya]]'' *''[[Young Jedi Knights: Delusions of Grandeur]]'' *''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos I: Hero's Trial]]'' ==Sources== *''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]'' {{1stID|3D-4X administrative droid}} {{1stp}} *{{GamerCite|3|Look Sir, Droids!}} *''[[Arms & Equipment Guide]]'' *{{FactFile|120|ADM 1-2|3D-4X Administrative Droid}} *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/livingforce/swlf20041216|text=Standoffs}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=3D-4X-Serie Administratordroide |nl=3D-4X Administrative Droid }} [[Category:Administrative droid models]] [[Category:Genetech Corporation products]] [[Category:Protocol droid models]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:3D-4X administrative droids]] }}
